stem: An isosceles right triangle has a perimeter of 62 + 62√2 inches. What is the length, in inches, of one leg of this triangle?
rationale: For an isosceles right triangle with legs of length L, the hypotenuse is L√2 and the perimeter is 2L + L√2. Setting 2L + L√2 = 62 + 62√2 and testing L = 31√2 gives 2(31√2) + (31√2)(√2) = 62√2 + 62 ✓. Therefore each leg has length 31√2. Choice A gives the wrong perimeter 62 + 31√2. Choice B and C correspond to the hypotenuse and a misreading.
